revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 走湯神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂495 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 伊豆奈比咩命 |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 走湯神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂495 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 伊豆奈比咩命 |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=走湯神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''走湯神社'''（すとうじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂（おおがも）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''伊豆奈比咩命神社'''（いつなひめのみことじんじゃ）に比定されており、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いつな姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm 走湯神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html 走湯神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 伊豆奈比咩命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:すとうしんしや しもたし}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 1 revision imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 走湯神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂495 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 伊豆奈比咩命 |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=走湯神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''走湯神社'''（すとうじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂（おおがも）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''伊豆奈比咩命神社'''（いつなひめのみことじんじゃ）に比定されており、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いつな姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm 走湯神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html 走湯神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 伊豆奈比咩命
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{デフォルトソート:すとうしんしや しもたし}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:下田市の建築物]] [[Category:下田市の歴史]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

'''走湯神社'''（すとうじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂（おおがも）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

== 概要 ==
下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''伊豆奈比咩命神社'''（いつなひめのみことじんじゃ）に比定されており、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いつな姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm 走湯神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html 走湯神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

== 祭神 ==
* 伊豆奈比咩命

== Japanese Wikipedia content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 走湯神社

{{coord2|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''走湯神社'''（すとうじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂（おおがも）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

== 概要 ==
下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''伊豆奈比咩命神社'''（いつなひめのみことじんじゃ）に比定されており、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いつな姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm 走湯神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html 走湯神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

== 祭神 ==
* 伊豆奈比咩命

== 脚注 ==
{{Reflist}}

== 外部リンク ==
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁

== Japanese Wikipedia content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 走湯神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}}}
|所在地 = [[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂495
|位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 伊豆奈比咩命
|社格   = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]]
|創建   =

|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=走湯神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''走湯神社'''（すとうじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[下田市]]大賀茂（おおがも）にある[[神社]]<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

== 概要 ==
下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、[[式内社]]である'''伊豆奈比咩命神社'''（いつなひめのみことじんじゃ）に比定されており、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 いつな姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm 走湯神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html 走湯神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

== 祭神 ==
* 伊豆奈比咩命

== 脚注 ==
{{Reflist}}

== 外部リンク ==
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁


==End Japanese==

[[Category:下田市の歴史]]
[[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 走湯神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=下田市}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}大賀茂495
|位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 伊豆奈比咩命
|社格   = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = 10月15日
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=走湯神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''走湯神社'''（すとうじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|下田市|Q653402}}大賀茂（おおがも）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 走湯神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
下田市の西方、吉佐美地区を流れる大賀茂川の北方上流、大賀茂地区の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、{{jalink|式内社}}である'''伊豆奈比咩命神社'''（いつなひめのみことじんじゃ）に比定されており、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 いつな姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm 走湯神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html 走湯神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 伊豆奈比咩命

=== 脚注 ===

=== 外部リンク ===

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

|Name = Sutō Shrine
|Image = {{Image requested|cat=Shimoda City}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|40|33.6|N|138|54|31.9|E}}}}

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{jalink|Shimoda City|Q653402}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{jalink|Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External Links ===
* [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

{{nihongo|'''Sutou Shrine'''|走湯神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto

'''Sutō Shrine''' (すとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ōgamo, {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401055 Sutō Shrine] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined at the foot of the mountains in the Ōgamo area, to the north and upstream of the Ōgamo River, which flows through the Kisami district west of Shimoda City.

Although the date of establishment is unknown, the shrine is identified with '''Izunahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いつなひめのみことじんじゃ), a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and is an ancient shrine recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}' as “Junior Fourth Rank, Upper: Itsunahime no Myōjin.”<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/sutou_title.htm Sutō Shrine] - Genmatsuko</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140112-01.html Sutō Shrine] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>

In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine.<ref name="engi"/>

=== Enshrined Deity ===
* Izunahime-no-Mikoto
